title: OER source-drift detection + refresh process
context: OER sources are updated — chapters revised, figures changed, learning objectives reworded — and banks aligned to a specific source version can become silently stale or incorrect. A wrong key that was once correct due to an outdated source value is the cardinal risk. The refresh process must detect when a bank's source version has changed, re-validate affected items, and route stale items as maintenance tasks. This process is a prerequisite for M3's sustainability goal.
objective: Document a source-drift detection and bank refresh process that detects OER source changes, re-validates affected items, and routes stale items as high-priority maintenance tasks.
acceptanceCriteria: Process documented: how to detect when a bank's OER source version has changed (e.g. checking source URL, edition, changelog, or SHA-256 of source content).
Re-validation step defined: which items are affected by a source change, how they are flagged, and how they become maintenance tasks.
Answer-key defect reports routed as high-priority maintenance with a named owner.
Process commits a re-validation artifact per bank recording the source version at last review and any drift detected.
Document committed to the project repo; CC-BY-4.0 licensed; commit DCO signed-off.
output: A committed source-drift detection and bank refresh process document, with re-validation artifact format, integrated into the project's maintenance workflow.
